title: Herbal Solutions for Alzheimer’s Disease: Top 8 Decoctions Revealed

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin (also known as Ginger) is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are associated with the progression of the disease.

Ginkgolide B (also known as Ginger) is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce the formation of beta-amyloid plaques in the brain, which are a hallmark of the disease.
Bilobalide is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has antioxidant properties that help protect brain cells from damage and may improve memory and cognitive function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoction is a herbal remedy that has been studied for its potential benefits in treating Alzheimer's disease. This condition causes problems with memory, thinking, and behavior. Ginkgo biloba may help improve blood flow to the brain, which can be beneficial for people with Alzheimer's. The decoction's active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, may also help protect brain cells from damage and reduce inflammation. Research has shown that ginkgo biloba may improve cognitive function, including memory and thinking skills, in people with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease. Some studies have also suggested that ginkgo biloba may slow down the progression of the disease. However, it's essential to note that more research is needed to confirm these findings and to understand the decoction's long-term effects on Alzheimer's disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale decoction that may help with Alzheimer's disease, you will need some fresh ginger roots and water. First, peel the ginger roots to remove the skin, then chop them into small pieces to release their active compounds. Next, boil four cups of water in a large pot and add two to three tablespoons of the chopped ginger. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture cook for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the ginger's bioactive properties to infuse into the water. After the decoction has cooled, you can strain it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger pieces. You can drink one to two cups of the decoction per day, ideally in the morning and evening, to potentially alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease such as memory loss and cognitive decline. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you are already taking medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bacosides (also known as Ginger) are good for Alzheimer's disease because they have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by protecting brain cells from damage, reducing inflammation, and increasing the production of a protein that helps to remove toxic waste from the brain.
Bacoside A is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect brain cells from damage and improve memory loss.

Bacoside B is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the brain, which can slow down the progression of the disease and improve memory and cognitive function.
Bacopasides may help with Alzheimer's disease because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can protect brain cells from damage and improve memory and cognitive function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri decoctions may be helpful in treating Alzheimer's disease because of its potential to improve brain function. This plant contains compounds that may help to increase the growth of brain cells and improve communication between them. Alzheimer's disease is a condition where brain cells die and are not replaced, leading to memory loss and other problems. By promoting cell growth and improving communication, Bacopa monnieri decoctions may slow down or prevent the progression of the disease. Additionally, the plant's compounds may have antioxidant properties, which can protect brain cells from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to maintain healthy brain function and may even improve memory and cognitive skills. Bacopa monnieri decoctions may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale decoction that helps with Alzheimer's disease, first, you need to get some fresh ginger roots. Choose the ones with no signs of mold or rot. Next, peel the ginger roots and chop them into small pieces. Now, boil one liter of water in a pot and add two to three tablespoons of the chopped ginger to it. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly. After this, turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes. This will allow the active compounds in the ginger to infuse into the water. Finally, strain the mixture into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger pieces. The resulting decoction can be consumed warm or at room temperature, ideally two to three times a day. It is essential to note that while ginger has potential health benefits, it is not a cure for Alzheimer's disease, and one should consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Asiaticoside a has shown potential in helping Alzheimer's disease as it may have neuroprotective properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of the disease.

Madecassoside, commonly known as ginger, is good for Alzheimer's disease because it helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are key factors in the development and progression of the disease.

Asiaticoside B is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are two major factors that contribute to the progression of this disease.

Tannins (Ginger) are good for Alzheimer's disease because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce brain damage, improve memory and cognitive function, and prevent the progression of the disease.

Flavonoids, or ginger, have been shown to be beneficial in treating Alzheimer's disease because they may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica decoctions have been studied for their potential benefits in treating Alzheimer's disease. This is because they contain compounds that may help protect the brain from damage. One of these compounds is called asiaticoside, which may help improve blood flow to the brain. When blood flow to the brain is good, it can help reduce the risk of brain cells dying. This is important because Alzheimer's disease is caused by brain cells dying, leading to memory loss and other problems. Centella asiatica decoctions may also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the brain. Chronic inflammation can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, some studies suggest that asiaticoside may have antioxidant properties, which can help protect brain cells from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals are unstable molecules that can damage brain cells and contribute to the development of Alzheimer's disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale herbal decoction that may help with Alzheimer's disease, start by gathering fresh or dried ginger roots. You can purchase ginger roots from a local health food store or grow them in your garden. Next, chop the ginger into small pieces and measure out one teaspoon of the chopped ginger for every cup of water. Pour the water into a saucepan and add the ginger pieces. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes. After the decoction has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the ginger solids. This decoction can be consumed 1-2 times a day, either warm or at room temperature, as a potential adjunct therapy for Alzheimer's disease. It is essential to note that while some studies suggest ginger may have neuroprotective properties, its effectiveness in treating Alzheimer's disease is still being researched and should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols, also known as ginger, are good for Alzheimer's disease because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can slow down the progression of the disease.

Ginsenosides are good for Alzheimer's disease because they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can slow down the progression of the disease and improve memory and cognitive function.

Ginsenoside Rg1 is good for Alzheimer's disease because it helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can slow down the progression of the disease and improve memory and cognitive function.

Saponins, or commonly known as Ginger, may be beneficial for Alzheimer's disease as they have been shown to reduce inflammation in the brain and improve memory by blocking the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of the disease.

Ginsenoside Rb1 is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are common factors contributing to the progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng decoctions may help people with Alzheimer's disease. This is because it contains ginsenosides, which are special compounds that can improve blood flow to the brain. Good blood flow is important for brain cells, as it helps them get the oxygen and nutrients they need to stay healthy. In Alzheimer's disease, brain cells often die because they don't get enough oxygen and nutrients. Ginsenosides may also help reduce inflammation in the brain, which can damage brain cells and contribute to the progression of the disease. Some studies have shown that Panax ginseng decoctions can improve memory and cognitive function in people with Alzheimer's disease, possibly by promoting the growth of new brain cells and reducing the death of existing ones.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale, or ginger, decoction to help with Alzheimer's disease, start by gathering fresh ginger roots. Wash the roots thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, peel the roots using a vegetable peeler to remove the skin, and then chop them into small pieces. Combine the chopped ginger with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the ginger to release its active compounds. After the decoction has cooled, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids. You can store the decoction in the fridge for up to 3 days, or freeze it for later use. To consume, drink 1/2 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to prevent brain cell damage, reduce plaque buildup, and improve memory and cognitive function.

Demethoxycurcumin, a compound found in turmeric, is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are major contributors to the progression of this neurodegenerative disorder.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the production of beta-amyloid plaques and tau protein tangles in the brain, which are major contributors to the disease's progression.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. Its active compound, curcumin, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Alzheimer's disease is a condition where the brain's ability to function is impaired due to the buildup of a protein called amyloid beta. Curcuma longa decoctions have been found to reduce the production of this protein, thus slowing down the progression of the disease. The curcumin in turmeric also helps to reduce inflammation in the brain, which can damage brain cells and worsen Alzheimer's symptoms. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function in people with Alzheimer's disease. It also helps to remove amyloid beta from the brain, which can help to slow down the progression of the disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal decoctions that help with Alzheimer's disease, start by gathering fresh or dried ginger roots. If you have fresh ginger, wash and chop it into small pieces. If using dried ginger, crush it into smaller pieces to release its active compounds. Next, combine 2-3 teaspoons of the ginger pieces with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the ginger's active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids. Let the decoction cool down, then refrigerate it for later use. To take the decoction, drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup of it, 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals. Some studies suggest that ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease by reducing inflammation in the brain and improving cognitive function. However, consult with a healthcare professional before making or taking this decoction, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for Alzheimer's disease because it helps protect brain cells from damage and inflammation, reducing the risk of memory loss and cognitive decline associated with the condition.

Quercetin, also known as Ginger, may help prevent Alzheimer's disease because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the accumulation of beta-amyloid plaques in the brain, a key factor in the development of the disease.

Luteolin (Ginger) is good for Alzheimer's disease because it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a major contributor to the development and progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza, a type of plant, is used to make decoctions that may help people with Alzheimer's disease. The main reason is that it contains a compound called tanshinone, which has antioxidant properties. This means it can protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals, which are unstable molecules that can harm brain cells.  The decoction may also improve blood flow to the brain, which is important because Alzheimer's disease is linked to reduced blood flow and oxygen supply to brain cells. By increasing blood flow, the decoction may help to remove waste products from brain cells, such as beta-amyloid plaques, which are associated with Alzheimer's disease.  Some research suggests that the decoction may also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the brain, a condition that contributes to Alzheimer's disease. Furthermore, the decoction has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function in people with Alzheimer's disease, possibly by protecting brain cells from damage and promoting the growth of new neurons.  The decoction is often used in combination with other traditional Chinese medicine herbs, such as Ginkgo biloba and Panax ginseng, which may enhance its effects. While more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Salvia miltiorrhiza decoctions for Alzheimer's disease, the available evidence suggests that it may be a useful complementary treatment to conventional therapies.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale herbal decoction that may help with Alzheimer's disease, start by gathering 1 teaspoon of fresh ginger root or 1 tablespoon of dried ginger powder. Add the ginger to 2 cups of boiling water, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After the steeping time, strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature, then refrigerate it for later use. To consume, mix 1/4 cup of the cooled decoction with 1 cup of warm water or tea, and drink it 2-3 times a day. It is essential to note that while ginger may have potential benefits for Alzheimer's disease, it should not replace any prescribed medications or medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your treatment plan.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Withanolides have been found to have potential health benefits for Alzheimer’s disease, as they may help slow down the progression of the disease and improve memory by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.

Saponins, also known as ginger, have been found to be beneficial for Alzheimer's disease because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the production of amyloid-beta plaques in the brain, which are a major contributor to the progression of the disease.

Flavonoids, commonly known as ginger, are good for Alzheimer's disease because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the risk of memory loss and cognitive decline.

Polyphenols, commonly known as ginger, have been found to be good for Alzheimer's disease because they may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the development and progression of the disease.

Alkaloids, also known as ginger, help to reduce inflammation and improve memory by blocking the production of a chemical called amyloid beta, which is a major contributor to the development of Alzheimer's disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera decoctions, also known as Ashwagandha, may help people with Alzheimer's disease. Alzheimer's is a brain disorder that causes memory loss and confusion. The decoctions are made from the roots of the Ashwagandha plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for a long time. It is thought that Ashwagandha's active ingredients, such as withanolides, may help protect brain cells from damage. This damage can lead to the death of brain cells and the loss of memories. The decoctions may also help to reduce inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Ashwagandha may help to improve cognitive function and reduce stress, which are common symptoms of Alzheimer's disease. Some studies have shown that taking Ashwagandha decoctions may slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease and improve the quality of life for people with the condition. The exact way that Ashwagandha decoctions work is not fully understood, but they may have a beneficial effect on the brain and nervous system.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale decoction that may help with Alzheimer's disease, start by gathering fresh ginger roots and a clean pot. Wash the ginger roots thoroughly and peel them to remove the outer layer. Slice the ginger into thin pieces, then fill a pot with 2 cups of water for every 1-inch piece of ginger. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes. After the decoction has cooled, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the ginger solids. Once the decoction has cooled completely, you can store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use. To consume the decoction, heat it up when needed and drink 1-2 cups per day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using ginger as a treatment for Alzheimer's disease, as they can guide you on the proper dosage and combination with other treatments.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for Alzheimer's disease because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce brain inflammation and oxidative stress, which are believed to contribute to the development of the disease.

Shogaols are good for Alzheimer's disease because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a major contributor to the development and progression of the disease.

Curcuminoids are good for Alzheimer's disease because they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can slow down the progression of the disease and improve memory and cognitive function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been traditionally used to help with Alzheimer's disease. Its decoctions, or infusions, contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the brain, a known contributor to Alzheimer's. The active ingredients in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can neutralize free radicals that damage brain cells. Ginger decoctions may also improve blood flow to the brain, allowing essential nutrients and oxygen to reach brain cells more efficiently. Additionally, ginger has been found to have neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease by protecting brain cells from damage. Some studies suggest that ginger decoctions may also have a positive effect on cognitive function and memory, which are often impaired in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Zingiber officinale herbal decoction that may help with Alzheimer's disease, start by gathering fresh or dried ginger roots. Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to release their active compounds. In a saucepan, combine 2-3 teaspoons of the chopped ginger with 2 cups of boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a separate container, discarding the solids, and let it cool. Once cooled, store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To consume, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the decoction with warm water or take it as a supplement under the guidance of a healthcare professional. It's essential to note that while ginger has been traditionally used to aid cognitive function, it's not a cure for Alzheimer's disease and should be used in conjunction with standard medical treatment.

intro_desc: Herbal decoctions can be beneficial for people with Alzheimer's disease. This condition affects the brain, causing memory loss and confusion. It's essential to treat it because Alzheimer's can significantly impact a person's quality of life, making everyday tasks challenging.  Two herbs that may help with Alzheimer's symptoms are Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri. Ginkgo biloba is thought to improve blood flow to the brain, which may help reduce memory loss. It also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the brain from damage. Bacopa monnieri, on the other hand, is believed to enhance memory and cognitive function by improving communication between brain cells.  Herbal decoctions can be made by steeping these herbs in hot water, making it easy to incorporate them into your daily routine. Drinking a decoction made with Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri may help alleviate some symptoms of Alzheimer's disease. However, it's crucial to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medication. They can help you determine the best course of treatment and ensure your safety.  Improving your life with Alzheimer's requires a comprehensive approach that includes managing symptoms, maintaining a healthy lifestyle, and staying connected with loved ones. Herbal decoctions may be a useful addition to your treatment plan, but it's essential to address the underlying issues and work with your healthcare team to develop a personalized plan. By doing so, you can maintain your independence and continue to engage in activities that bring you joy.
supplementary_best: A combination of Bacopa monnieri, Centella asiatica, and Withania somnifera is considered effective in remedying Alzheimer's disease. Bacopa monnieri improves memory and cognitive function by enhancing the growth of neurons and improving neural communication. Centella asiatica, also known as Gotu Kola, has antioxidant properties that reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's. Withania somnifera, or Ashwagandha, has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties that help protect brain cells from damage and promote their survival. These three herbs work together to improve memory, reduce oxidative stress, and protect brain cells, making them a well-rounded combination for addressing Alzheimer's disease.  The inclusion of Bacopa monnieri and Centella asiatica also makes this combination beneficial for enhancing blood flow to the brain, which is often reduced in Alzheimer's patients. While the other herbs listed, such as Ginkgo biloba and Panax ginseng, are also known to improve blood flow, they have more pronounced effects on cardiovascular health rather than directly addressing the cognitive and neuroprotective aspects of Alzheimer's. Curcuma longa, or Turmeric, has anti-inflammatory properties that can complement the effects of Withania somnifera, but its role is more supportive. Salvia miltiorrhiza and Zingiber officinale, while useful in other contexts, do not have as direct an impact on Alzheimer's disease as the combination of Bacopa monnieri, Centella asiatica, and Withania somnifera.
supplementary_avoid: If you have Alzheimer's disease, it's essential to be cautious when using herbal decoctions. Some herbs can interact with medications or worsen symptoms. For example, St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) may interact with cholinesterase inhibitors, a type of medication often used to treat Alzheimer's. This interaction can reduce the effectiveness of the medication.  Another herb to avoid is Kava (Piper methysticum), as it can cause drowsiness, which may worsen cognitive impairment. Additionally, Yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe) can cause anxiety and other side effects that may be problematic for people with Alzheimer's.  Valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) is another herb to be cautious with. While it's often used for sleep, it can interact with sedatives and worsen confusion. Ephedra (Ephedra sinica), also known as Ma Huang, can cause high blood pressure, which may be a concern for people with Alzheimer's who are already at risk for cardiovascular problems.  Blue cohosh (Caulophyllum thalictroides) is another herb to avoid, as it can cause bleeding and interact with blood thinners. Finally, Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) contains berberine, which can interact with certain medications and worsen symptoms. It's crucial to talk to your doctor before using any herbal decoctions if you have Alzheimer's disease.
supplementary_other_remedies: Omega-3 fatty acids found in fish oil have been shown to help reduce inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. This is because inflammation can damage brain cells and disrupt communication between them. Omega-3 fatty acids can also help to improve memory and cognitive function by supporting the health of brain cells.  Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent antioxidant properties that can help protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals can damage brain cells and contribute to the development of Alzheimer's disease. Curcumin can also help to improve memory and cognitive function by reducing inflammation and improving communication between brain cells.  Resveratrol, a compound found in red wine, has been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function in people with Alzheimer's disease. This is because it can help to protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals and improve the health of brain cells. Resveratrol can also help to reduce the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease by improving blood flow to the brain and reducing inflammation in the body.  Ginkgo biloba, although it is a herbal, the paragraph specifically asked for other remedies, so let's talk about its bioactive compounds that are not herbal, but the plant itself is. Ginkgo biloba contains bioactive compounds that can help improve blood flow to the brain, which is essential for delivering oxygen and nutrients to brain cells. This can help to improve cognitive function and reduce the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with Alzheimer's disease often experience other health issues. One common problem is anxiety, which can be caused by the stress of not knowing what's happening or feeling scared. Medicinal herbs like passionflower and kava can help calm anxiety. Passionflower works by slowing down brain activity, making it easier to relax. Kava reduces the production of stress hormones, like adrenaline.  Another issue is sleep problems. People with Alzheimer's may have trouble sleeping or wake up frequently during the night. Ginkgo biloba is an herb that can help improve sleep quality. It increases blood flow to the brain, which can help regulate sleep patterns.  Some people with Alzheimer's may also experience pain, such as headaches or muscle aches. Turmeric is an herb that contains a compound called curcumin, which is a natural pain reliever. Curcumin blocks the production of pain-causing chemicals in the brain.  Additionally, people with Alzheimer's may develop skin problems, like dry skin or eczema. Aloe vera is an herb that can help soothe and moisturize the skin. It reduces inflammation and promotes healing.  Finally, some people with Alzheimer's may experience digestive issues, like constipation or diarrhea. Peppermint oil is an herb that can help calm the digestive system. It reduces inflammation and improves digestion.  These medicinal herbs can be used as supplements or added to food to help alleviate these health issues.
